items.name: Headscale
items.summary: Self-hosted Tailscale control server for private networks.
items.why_included: Iroh commenters pointed to it as the fully OSS route for Tailscale-style setups.
items.evidence: A commenter said that if you use Headscale, then every part of the Tailscale stack is OSS.

items.name: Bifrost
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: Has anyone replaced Claude/GPT with a local model for daily coding?
items.summary: LLM gateway people use as the front door to their model stack.
items.why_included: A commenter set it up as the primary access point for served models.
items.evidence: One commenter said they recently set up Bifrost, an LLM gateway, as the primary access point for the models they serve.

items.name: Open WebUI
items.source_story_title: Ask HN: Has anyone replaced Claude/GPT with a local model for daily coding?
items.summary: Self-hosted AI interface for local and cloud models.
items.why_included: It showed up as part of a real local-model stack people are actively using.
items.evidence: A commenter said they connect Open WebUI alongside other applications in their model setup.

items.name: OpenRouter Fusion
items.summary: Multi-model routing and deliberation for harder prompts.
items.why_included: Commenters tried the budget and quality modes and treated it as a real option.
items.evidence: People said they tried OpenRouter Fusion in budget mode and discussed its multi-model quorum behavior and costs.

items.name: Iroh
items.summary: Peer-to-peer networking stack for direct device-to-device connections.
items.why_included: Commenters said they use it in production and like the app-layer model.
items.evidence: Several commenters praised Iroh as a production library and said the docs, custom transports, and app-layer connectivity model are compelling.

items.name: machine0
items.source_story_title: Show HN: machine0 – Persistent NixOS VMs You Control from the CLI
items.summary: Persistent CLI VMs with NixOS/Ubuntu and preinstalled tools.
items.why_included: Commenters are already running real apps on it and pointing agents at its VMs.
items.evidence: One commenter said to point an agent at a machine0 VM; another said they were already hosting openclaw and web apps on it.

items.name: fata
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Fata – Spaced repetition to fight skill rot from AI coding
items.summary: Spaced-repetition app for keeping coding fundamentals sharp.
items.why_included: A commenter said they downloaded it; others treated it as useful practice infrastructure.
items.evidence: A commenter said they downloaded the app and would spend time using it, while others said fata helps practice regularly.

items.name: Reps
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Fata – Spaced repetition to fight skill rot from AI coding
items.summary: Daily coding puzzle for practicing skills and fighting skill rot.
items.why_included: A commenter surfaced it as a concrete daily practice tool.
items.evidence: A commenter introduced Reps as a daily coding puzzle built to help fight skill rot.
